title: A Fossil of a New Carnivoran Species Effectively Doubles the Evolutionary History of the Weasel Family
description: A new study doubles the evolutionary history of the weasel family. Researchers, including Chris Law, a University of Washington principal research scientist in the biology department, have determined that a fossil that was discovered in Spain belongs to a new species dating back to around 6.5 million years ago. This new species was likely similar in size to the smallest living weasel species today, the least weasel.

title: Why April 15 marks Thailand’s true New Year in the ancient tradition
description: April 15, known as Wan Thaloeng Sok or Wan Phaya Wan, marks the true start of the Thai New Year in ancient belief and Lanna tradition

title: Ancient Nang Dan rite keeps Nakhon Si Thammarat’s Songkran spirit alive
description: Nakhon Si Thammarat’s 1,200-year-old Nang Dan procession returns as a rare Songkran ritual blending Brahmin heritage, sacred symbolism and local faith.

title: Why more fossil fuels won’t fix the Iran energy crisis
description: Nature, Published online: 15 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01197-1 Climate-friendly technologies are the best way to stymie rising inflation — and will get better and cheaper over time.

title: ‘A ray of hope’: EU governments gathering to plan way out of fossil fuel reliance
description: Energy crises could be a thing of the past if reliable, cheap and abundant clean energy is given precedence over fossil fuels.
